title: Sweet Kenyan Lotus Leaf Wrap
description: A romantic and sweet Kenyan dish featuring Thai bird chili and nigiri-shaped ingredients wrapped in a lotus leaf. This whole30-friendly dish is perfect for a dinner date.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the shredded coconut and cook, stirring frequently, until it is lightly toasted and fragrant.
In a small bowl, mix together the coconut sugar, nutmeg, and a pinch of salt.
Add the Thai bird chili to the bowl and mix until well combined.
Unroll the lotus leaves and cut out four circles using a cookie cutter or a knife.
Place a tablespoon of the coconut sugar mixture on the center of each lotus leaf circle.
Arrange 3 shrimp on top of the coconut sugar mixture, followed by a sprinkle of pumpkin seeds.
Fold the lotus leaves over the filling, forming a triangle or a square shape, and press the edges together to seal the wrap.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and wrappers.
Steam the wraps over boiling water for 10-12 minutes, or until the leaves are tender and pliable.
Serve the wraps warm, garnished with additional pumpkin seeds and a sprinkle of coconut sugar.
